name: Pizza Hat Crust
recipeInstructions:
1. Sprinkle yeast over warm water and stir in the sugar. Let stand about 5 minutes or until very bubbly.
2. Combine the remaining ingredients with about half of the flour, beating to a smooth batter.
3. Beat in the yeast mixture and then work in the remaining flour with a sturdy spoon until you can toss it lightly on a floured surface and knead it until it feels elastic in texture. The kneading may require about 2/3 cup additional flour, which you will coat your hands with as you knead the dough.
4. Don't let the dough become too stiff, but don't want it to stick to your hands either.
5. Place the dough in a large plastic food bag, spraying inside with Pam or wiping with oil. Ensure the bag is large enough for the dough to double without splitting.
6. Place the bag of dough on a warm, sunny spot on the table or kitchen counter to rise quickly. If it's summertime, place the bag of dough (with open end sealed tightly with tape) in your car with windows up.
7. When the dough has doubled, punch it down and shape it to fit two 15-inch round pizza pans that have been greased and dusted with cornmeal.
8. Add the sauce and topping ingredients exactly as listed above, spreading each evenly over the dough.
9. Let this rise about 20 minutes in a warm place.
10. Bake at 450°F for about 20 to 25 minutes, placing one pizza at a time on the center rack of the preheated oven.
11. If you don't want a full pizza and can only handle one, wrap the second pizza in foil or plastic and seal it with tape to lock out all air. Freeze before the last rising occurs.
12. After letting it thaw for 30 minutes, bake as directed above.
